title: [lichen_l2.c:203-204] tx_ipv6_buf and tx_frame_buf sizes assume no IPv6 extension headers
description:
Category not checked: Buffer sizing assumptions vs protocol reality

Bug found: tx_ipv6_buf is sized as LICHEN_L2_MTU + IPV6_BASE_HDR_LEN (200 + 40 = 240 bytes). The comment at lines 191-201 documents this limitation but doesn't guard against it.

If IPv6 extension headers are present (Hop-by-Hop, Routing, Destination, OSCORE, etc.), the packet can exceed 240 bytes before SCHC compression. The code at line 552-554 rejects packets > sizeof(tx_ipv6_buf), but the error message says 'Packet too large' without indicating the extension header limitation.

More critically: Zephyr's IPv6 stack CAN send packets with extension headers. The MTU is 200 bytes, but that's the payload after IPv6 header - a packet with a 40-byte base header + 8-byte routing header + 192-byte payload = 240 bytes would fit the MTU but exceed the buffer.

Impact: P3 - Silent packet drops with confusing error when using extension headers, which the FIXME(OSCORE) hints will be needed.
close_reason: Fixed: Documented that IPv6 extension headers are not supported by design. Added explicit LIMITATION comment explaining TX path fails with -EMSGSIZE, RX path fails size check. This is intentional because SCHC rules target CoAP/ICMPv6/RPL without extension headers. Extension headers fall back to rule 255 (uncompressed) which exceeds MTU.
